member.name: T:Unity.VisualScripting.Variables
member.summary: Component that handles the variables that you can use in the Script Graphs of your project.

remarks.para: This component is automatically added to a GameObject when a is added, or when (Script) are created using the “Visual Scripting Scene Variables” menu in the Hierarchy.

The component manages the object variables for any GameObject that you add it to. You can access these variables from the Object Variables section of the Blackboard of any associated with the GameObject.

The component also manages the scene variables for any “VisualScripting SceneVariables” GameObject that you add it to. You can access these scene variables from the Scene Variables section of the Blackboard of any you add to this same scene.

Note: It's best not to add a component to a GameObject that also includes the (Script). If you do this, the Blackboard displays Scene Variables that are also present in Object Variables and this can cause confusion.
For more information on how to interact with Variables, refer to the User Manual.

example.para: The following example shows how to programmatically change the value of a variable used in a Visual Scripting graph. Every time you press the Space key, we double the value of the velocity variable. Note: You can try this example in a Script Graph.

member.name: P:Unity.VisualScripting.Variables.declarations
member.summary: Retrieves a collection of the variables set in the Variables component.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.Variables.Graph(Unity.VisualScripting.GraphPointer)
member.summary: Retrieves a collection of graph variables for a given graph.
member.param: The reference to a graph.
member.returns: If the graph is instantiated, returns the graph variables of that instantiated graph. Otherwise, returns the graph variables from the definition of the given graph (i.e.: from the graph asset definition)

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.Variables.GraphInstance(Unity.VisualScripting.GraphPointer)
member.summary: Retrieves a collection of graph variables of an instantiated graph.
member.param: The reference to a graph.
member.returns: A collection of graph variables of an instantiated graph.
member.exception: If the graph data cannot be read. Which probably means that the graph is not instantiated.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.Variables.GraphDefinition(Unity.VisualScripting.GraphPointer)
member.summary: Retrieves a collection of graph variables from the definition of a graph.
member.param: The reference to a graph.
member.returns: A collection of graph variables of a given graph.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.Variables.GraphDefinition(Unity.VisualScripting.IGraphWithVariables)
member.summary: Retrieves a collection of graph variables from the definition of a graph.
member.param: The reference of a graph
member.returns: A collection of graph variables of a given graph.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.Variables.Object(UnityEngine.GameObject)
member.summary: Retrieves a collection of the object variables of a given .
member.param: The whose object variables will be returned.
member.returns: A collection of the object variables contained in the Variables component of the that was passed as a parameter.
member.remarks: If the doesn't have a Variables component, it is supplied with one by default and the returned collection is empty.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.Variables.Object(UnityEngine.Component)
member.summary: Retrieves a collection of the object variables of a given .
member.param: The whose 's object variables are returned.
member.returns: A collection of object variables contained in the Variables component of the 's .
member.remarks: If the GameObject does not have a Variables component, a Variables component is added to the and the returned collection is empty

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.Variables.Scene(System.Nullable{UnityEngine.SceneManagement.Scene})
member.summary: Retrieves a collection of scene variables for a given Scene.
member.param: The Scene whose scene variables are returned.
member.returns: A collection of scene variables contained in the Variables component associated with the (Script).

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.Variables.Scene(UnityEngine.GameObject)
member.summary: Retrieves a collection of scene variables for a given Scene.
member.param: A whose Scene will be accessed to get its variables.
member.returns: A collection of scene variables contained in the Variables component associated with the (Script).

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.Variables.Scene(UnityEngine.Component)
member.summary: Retrieves a collection of scene variables for a given Scene.
member.param: A whose 's Scene's scene variables will be returned.
member.returns: A collection of scene variables contained in the Variables component associated with the (Script).

member.name: P:Unity.VisualScripting.Variables.ActiveScene
member.summary: Retrieve a collection of the scene variables of the active scene.

member.name: P:Unity.VisualScripting.Variables.Application
member.summary: Retrieve a collection of the application variables.

member.name: P:Unity.VisualScripting.Variables.Saved
member.summary: Retrieve a collection of the saved variables.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.Variables.ExistOnObject(UnityEngine.GameObject)
member.summary: Check if a Variables component exists on the passed as a parameter.
member.param: The we want to check for the Variables component.
member.returns: True if the has a Variables component. Otherwise, returns false.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.Variables.ExistOnObject(UnityEngine.Component)
member.summary: Check if a Variables component exists on the 's passed as a parameter.
member.param: A for which we want to know if the has a Variables component.
member.returns: True if the 's has a Variables component. Otherwise, returns false.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.Variables.ExistInScene(System.Nullable{UnityEngine.SceneManagement.Scene})
member.summary: Check if there is a SceneVariables component instantiated to find out if the scene contains scene variables.
member.param: A Scene we want to check for scene variables.
member.returns: True if the Scene is not null and contains scene variables. Otherwise, returns false.

member.name: P:Unity.VisualScripting.Variables.ExistInActiveScene
member.summary: Check if there is a SceneVariables component instantiated to find out if the active scene contains scene variables.
member.remarks: Returns true if the active scene contains scene variables. Otherwise, returns false.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.Variables.GetAotStubs(System.Collections.Generic.HashSet{System.Object})
member.summary: Don't use this method. It is for Unity Visual Scripting internal usage only.

member.name: T:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.Internal.fsPortableReflection
member.summary: This wraps reflection types so that it is portable across different Unity runtimes.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.Internal.fsPortableReflection.HasAttribute``1(System.Reflection.MemberInfo)
member.summary: Returns true if the given attribute is defined on the given element.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.Internal.fsPortableReflection.HasAttribute``1(System.Reflection.MemberInfo,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Returns true if the given attribute is defined on the given element.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.Internal.fsPortableReflection.HasAttribute(System.Reflection.MemberInfo,System.Type)
member.summary: Returns true if the given attribute is defined on the given element.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.Internal.fsPortableReflection.HasAttribute(System.Reflection.MemberInfo,System.Type,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Returns true if the given attribute is defined on the given element.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.Internal.fsPortableReflection.GetAttribute(System.Reflection.MemberInfo,System.Type,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Fetches the given attribute from the given MemberInfo. This method applies caching and is allocation free (after caching has been performed).
member.param: The MemberInfo the get the attribute from.
The type of attribute to fetch.
member.returns: The attribute or null.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.Internal.fsPortableReflection.GetAttribute``1(System.Reflection.MemberInfo,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Fetches the given attribute from the given MemberInfo.
member.typeparam: The type of attribute to fetch.
member.param: The MemberInfo to get the attribute from.
Should this computation be cached? If this is the only time it will ever be done, don't bother caching.
member.returns: The attribute or null.

member.name: F:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.Internal.fsVersionedType.Ancestors
member.summary: The direct ancestors that this type can import.

member.name: F:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.Internal.fsVersionedType.VersionString
member.summary: The identifying string that is unique among all ancestors.

member.name: F:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.Internal.fsVersionedType.ModelType
member.summary: The modeling type that this versioned type maps back to.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.Internal.fsVersionedType.Migrate(System.Object)
member.summary: Migrate from an instance of an ancestor.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.Internal.fsVersionManager.VerifyConstructors(Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.Internal.fsVersionedType)
member.summary: Verifies that the given type has constructors to migrate from all ancestor types.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.Internal.fsVersionManager.VerifyUniqueVersionStrings(Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.Internal.fsVersionedType)
member.summary: Verifies that the given version graph contains only unique versions.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.Internal.fsTypeExtensions.CSharpName(System.Type)
member.summary: Returns a pretty name for the type in the style of one that you'd see in C# without the namespace.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.Internal.fsTypeExtensions.CSharpName(System.Type,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Returns a pretty name for the type in the style of one that you'd see in C#.
member.param: Should the name include namespaces?

member.name: T:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.Internal.fsOption`1
member.summary: Simple option type. This is akin to nullable types.

member.name: T:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsPropertyAttribute
member.summary: Explicitly mark a property to be serialized. This can also be used to give the name that the property should use during serialization.

member.name: F:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsPropertyAttribute.Name
member.summary: The name of that the property will use in JSON serialization.

member.name: F:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsPropertyAttribute.Converter
member.summary: Use a custom converter for the given type. Specify the converter to use using typeof.

member.name: T:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsDataType
member.summary: The actual type that a JsonData instance can store.

member.name: T:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsData
member.summary: A union type that stores a serialized value. The stored type can be one of six different types: null, boolean, double, Int64, string, Dictionary, or List.

member.name: F:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsData._value
member.summary: The raw value that this serialized data stores. It can be one of six different types; a boolean, a double, Int64, a string, a Dictionary, or a List.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsData.#ctor
member.summary: Creates a fsData instance that holds null.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsData.#ctor(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Creates a fsData instance that holds a boolean.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsData.#ctor(System.Double)
member.summary: Creates a fsData instance that holds a double.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsData.#ctor(System.Int64)
member.summary: Creates a new fsData instance that holds an integer.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsData.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Creates a fsData instance that holds a string.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsData.#ctor(System.Collections.Generic.Dictionary{System.String,Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsData})
member.summary: Creates a fsData instance that holds a dictionary of values.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsData.#ctor(System.Collections.Generic.List{Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsData})
member.summary: Creates a fsData instance that holds a list of values.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsData.CreateDictionary
member.summary: Helper method to create a fsData instance that holds a dictionary.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsData.CreateList
member.summary: Helper method to create a fsData instance that holds a list.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsData.CreateList(System.Int32)
member.summary: Helper method to create a fsData instance that holds a list with the initial capacity.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsData.BecomeDictionary
member.summary: Transforms the internal fsData instance into a dictionary.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsData.Clone
member.summary: Returns a shallow clone of this data instance.

member.name: P:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsData.IsNull
member.summary: Returns true if this fsData instance maps back to null.

member.name: P:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsData.IsDouble
member.summary: Returns true if this fsData instance maps back to a double.

member.name: P:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsData.IsInt64
member.summary: Returns true if this fsData instance maps back to an Int64.

member.name: P:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsData.IsBool
member.summary: Returns true if this fsData instance maps back to a boolean.

member.name: P:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsData.IsString
member.summary: Returns true if this fsData instance maps back to a string.

member.name: P:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsData.IsDictionary
member.summary: Returns true if this fsData instance maps back to a Dictionary.

member.name: P:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsData.IsList
member.summary: Returns true if this fsData instance maps back to a List.

member.name: P:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsData.AsDouble
member.summary: Casts this fsData to a double. Throws an exception if it is not a double.

member.name: P:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsData.AsInt64
member.summary: Casts this fsData to an Int64. Throws an exception if it is not an Int64.

member.name: P:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsData.AsBool
member.summary: Casts this fsData to a boolean. Throws an exception if it is not a boolean.

member.name: P:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsData.AsString
member.summary: Casts this fsData to a string. Throws an exception if it is not a string.

member.name: P:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsData.AsDictionary
member.summary: Casts this fsData to a Dictionary. Throws an exception if it is not a Dictionary.

member.name: P:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsData.AsList
member.summary: Casts this fsData to a List. Throws an exception if it is not a List.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsData.Cast``1
member.summary: Internal helper method to cast the underlying storage to the given type or throw a pretty printed exception on failure.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsData.Equals(System.Object)
member.summary: Determines whether the specified object is equal to the current object.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsData.Equals(Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsData)
member.summary: Determines whether the specified object is equal to the current object.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsData.op_Equality(Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsData,Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsData)
member.summary: Returns true iff a == b.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsData.op_Inequality(Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsData,Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsData)
member.summary: Returns true iff a != b.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsData.GetHashCode
member.summary: Returns a hash code for this instance.
member.returns: A hash code for this instance, suitable for use in hashing algorithms and data structures like a hash table.

member.name: T:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsTypeCache
member.summary: Caches type name to type lookups. Type lookups occur in all loaded assemblies.

member.name: T:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsBaseConverter
member.summary: The serialization converter allows for customization of the serialization process.
member.remarks: You do not want to derive from this class - there is no way to actually use it within the serializer.. Instead, derive from either fsConverter or fsDirectConverter

member.name: F:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsBaseConverter.Serializer
member.summary: The serializer that was owns this converter.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsBaseConverter.CreateInstance(Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsData,System.Type)
member.summary: Construct an object instance that will be passed to TryDeserialize. This should **not** deserialize the object.
member.param: The data the object was serialized with.
The field/property type that is storing the instance.
member.returns: An object instance

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsBaseConverter.RequestCycleSupport(System.Type)
member.summary: If true, then the serializer will support cyclic references with the given converted type.
member.param: The field/property type that is currently storing the object that is being serialized.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsBaseConverter.RequestInheritanceSupport(System.Type)
member.summary: If true, then the serializer will include inheritance data for the given converter.
member.param: The field/property type that is currently storing the object that is being serialized.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsBaseConverter.TrySerialize(System.Object,Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsData@,System.Type)
member.summary: Serialize the actual object into the given data storage.
member.param: The object instance to serialize. This will never be null.
The serialized state.
The field/property type that is storing this instance.
member.returns: If serialization was successful.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsBaseConverter.TryDeserialize(Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsData,System.Object@,System.Type)
member.summary: Deserialize data into the object instance.
member.param: Serialization data to deserialize from.
The object instance to deserialize into.
The field/property type that is storing the instance.
member.returns: True if serialization was successful, false otherwise.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsJsonPrinter.InsertSpacing(System.IO.TextWriter,System.Int32)
member.summary: Inserts the given number of indents into the builder.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsJsonPrinter.EscapeString(System.String)
member.summary: Escapes a string.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsJsonPrinter.BuildPrettyString(Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsData,System.IO.TextWriter,System.Int32)
member.summary: Formats this data into the given builder.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsJsonPrinter.PrettyJson(Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsData,System.IO.TextWriter)
member.summary: Writes the pretty JSON output data to the given stream.
member.param: The data to print.
Where to write the printed data.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsJsonPrinter.PrettyJson(Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsData)
member.summary: Returns the data in a pretty printed JSON format.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsJsonPrinter.CompressedJson(Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsData,System.IO.StreamWriter)
member.summary: Writes the compressed JSON output data to the given stream.
member.param: The data to print.
Where to write the printed data.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsJsonPrinter.CompressedJson(Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsData)
member.summary: Returns the data in a relatively compressed JSON format.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsJsonPrinter.ConvertDoubleToString(System.Double)
member.summary: Utility method that converts a double to a string.

member.name: T:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsGuidConverter
member.summary: Serializes and deserializes guids.

member.name: T:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsConverterRegistrar
member.summary: This class allows arbitrary code to easily register global converters. To add a converter, simply declare a new field called "Register_*" that stores the type of converter you would like to add. Alternatively, you can do the same with a method called "Register_*"; just add the converter type to the `Converters` list.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.InputAction_DirectConverter.MakeInputActionWithId(System.String,System.String,System.String,UnityEngine.InputSystem.InputActionType)
member.summary: Creates a fake InputAction. Ports with an editor MUST serialize a value of the port's type, even if a GUID would suffice in that case

summary.para: Enables injecting code before/after an object has been serialized. This is most useful if you want to run the default serialization process but apply a pre/post processing step.

Multiple object processors can be active at the same time. When running they are called in a "nested" fashion - if we have processor1 and process2 added to the serializer in that order (p1 then p2), then the execution order will be p1#Before p2#Before /serialization/ p2#After p1#After.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsObjectProcessor.CanProcess(System.Type)
member.summary: Is the processor interested in objects of the given type?
member.param: The given type.
member.returns: True if the processor should be applied, false otherwise.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsObjectProcessor.OnBeforeSerialize(System.Type,System.Object)
member.summary: Called before serialization.
member.param: The field/property type that is storing the instance.
The type of the instance.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsObjectProcessor.OnAfterSerialize(System.Type,System.Object,Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsData@)
member.summary: Called after serialization.
member.param: The field/property type that is storing the instance.
The type of the instance.
The data that was serialized.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsObjectProcessor.OnBeforeDeserialize(System.Type,Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsData@)
member.summary: Called before deserialization.
member.param: The field/property type that is storing the instance.
The data that will be used for deserialization.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsObjectProcessor.OnBeforeDeserializeAfterInstanceCreation(System.Type,System.Object,Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsData@)
member.summary: Called before deserialization has begun but *after* the object instance has been created. This will get invoked even if the user passed in an existing instance.
member.remarks: **IMPORTANT**: The actual instance that gets passed here is *not* guaranteed to be an a subtype of storageType, since the value for instance is whatever the active converter returned for CreateInstance() - ie, some converters will return dummy types in CreateInstance() if instance creation cannot be separated from deserialization (ie, KeyValuePair).
member.param: The field/property type that is storing the instance.
The created object instance. No deserialization has been applied to it.
The data that will be used for deserialization.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsObjectProcessor.OnAfterDeserialize(System.Type,System.Object)
member.summary: Called after deserialization.
member.param: The field/property type that is storing the instance.
The type of the instance.

member.name: T:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsJsonParser
member.summary: A simple recursive descent parser for JSON.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsJsonParser.SkipSpace
member.summary: Skips input such that Character() will return a non-whitespace character

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsJsonParser.TryParseNumber(Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsData@)
member.summary: Parses numbers that follow the regular expression [-+](\d+|\d*\.\d*)

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsJsonParser.TryParseString(System.String@)
member.summary: Parses a string

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsJsonParser.TryParseArray(Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsData@)
member.summary: Parses an array

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsJsonParser.Parse(System.String,Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsData@)
member.summary: Parses the specified input. Returns a failure state if parsing failed.
member.param: The input to parse.
The parsed data. This is undefined if parsing fails.
member.returns: The parsed input.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsJsonParser.Parse(System.String)
member.summary: Helper method for Parse that does not allow the error information to be recovered.

member.name: T:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsForwardAttribute
M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsForwardAttribute.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Forward object serialization to an instance member. See class comment.
member.param: The name of the member that we should serialize this object as.

member.name: F:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsForwardAttribute.MemberName
member.summary: The name of the member we should serialize as.

member.name: T:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsIgnoreAttribute
member.summary: The given property or field annotated with [JsonIgnore] will not be serialized.

member.name: T:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsConverter
member.summary: The serialization converter allows for customization of the serialization process.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsConverter.CanProcess(System.Type)
member.summary: Can this converter serialize and deserialize the given object type?
member.param: The given object type.
member.returns: True if the converter can serialize it, false otherwise.

member.name: T:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsDirectConverter
member.summary: The direct converter is similar to a regular converter, except that it targets specifically only one type. This means that it can be used without performance impact when discovering converters. It is strongly recommended that you derive from fsDirectConverter{TModel}.
member.remarks: Due to the way that direct converters operate, inheritance is *not* supported. Direct converters will only be used with the exact ModelType object.

member.name: T:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsISerializationCallbacks
member.summary: Extend this interface on your type to receive notifications about serialization/deserialization events. If you don't have access to the type itself, then you can write an fsObjectProcessor instead.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsISerializationCallbacks.OnBeforeSerialize(System.Type)
member.summary: Called before serialization.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsISerializationCallbacks.OnAfterSerialize(System.Type,Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsData@)
member.summary: Called after serialization.
member.param: The field/property type that is storing the instance.
The data that was serialized.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsISerializationCallbacks.OnBeforeDeserialize(System.Type,Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsData@)
member.summary: Called before deserialization.
member.param: The field/property type that is storing the instance.
The data that will be used for deserialization.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsISerializationCallbacks.OnAfterDeserialize(System.Type)
member.summary: Called after deserialization.
member.param: The field/property type that is storing the instance.

member.name: T:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsMetaProperty
member.summary: A property or field on a MetaType. This unifies the FieldInfo and PropertyInfo classes.

member.name: F:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsMetaProperty._memberInfo
member.summary: Internal handle to the reflected member.

member.name: P:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsMetaProperty.StorageType
member.summary: The type of value that is stored inside of the property. For example, for an int field, StorageType will be typeof(int).

member.name: P:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsMetaProperty.OverrideConverterType
member.summary: A custom fsBaseConverter instance to use for this field/property, if requested. This will be null if the default converter selection algorithm should be used. This is specified using the [fsObject] annotation with the Converter field.

member.name: P:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsMetaProperty.CanRead
member.summary: Can this property be read?

member.name: P:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsMetaProperty.CanWrite
member.summary: Can this property be written to?

member.name: P:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsMetaProperty.JsonName
member.summary: The serialized name of the property, as it should appear in JSON.

member.name: P:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsMetaProperty.MemberName
member.summary: The name of the actual member.

member.name: P:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsMetaProperty.IsPublic
member.summary: Is this member public?

member.name: P:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsMetaProperty.IsReadOnly
member.summary: Is this type readonly? We can modify readonly properties using reflection, but not using generated C#.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsMetaProperty.Write(System.Object,System.Object)
member.summary: Writes a value to the property that this MetaProperty represents, using given object instance as the context.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsMetaProperty.Read(System.Object)
member.summary: Reads a value from the property that this MetaProperty represents, using the given object instance as the context.

member.name: T:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsEnumConverter
member.summary: Serializes and deserializes enums by their current name.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsEnumConverter.ArrayContains``1(``0[],``0)
member.summary: Returns true if the given value is contained within the specified array.

member.name: T:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsObjectAttribute
member.summary: This attribute controls some serialization behavior for a type. See the comments on each of the fields for more information.

member.name: F:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsObjectAttribute.PreviousModels
member.summary: The previous model that should be used if an old version of this object is encountered. Using this attribute also requires that the type have a public constructor that takes only one parameter, an object instance of the given type. Use of this parameter *requires* that the VersionString parameter is also set.

member.name: F:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsObjectAttribute.VersionString
member.summary: The version string to use for this model. This should be unique among all prior versions of this model that is supported for importation. If PreviousModel is set, then this attribute must also be set. A good valid example for this is "v1", "v2", "v3", ...

member.name: F:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsObjectAttribute.MemberSerialization
member.summary: This controls the behavior for member serialization. The default behavior is fsMemberSerialization.Default.

member.name: F:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsObjectAttribute.Converter
member.summary: Specify a custom converter to use for serialization. The converter type needs to derive from fsBaseConverter. This defaults to null.

member.name: F:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsObjectAttribute.Processor
member.summary: Specify a custom processor to use during serialization. The processor type needs to derive from fsObjectProcessor and the call to CanProcess is not invoked. This defaults to null.

member.name: T:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsAotCompilationManager
member.summary: The AOT compilation manager

member.name: P:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsAotCompilationManager.AvailableAotCompilations
member.summary:
Ahead of time compilations that are available. The type maps to the object type the generated converter will serialize/deserialize, and the string is the text content for a converter that will do the serialization.
The generated serializer is completely independent and you don't need to do anything. Simply add the file to your project and it'll get used instead of the reflection based one.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsAotCompilationManager.TryToPerformAotCompilation(Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsConfig,System.Type,System.String@)
member.summary: This is a helper method that makes it simple to run an AOT compilation on the given type.
member.param: The configuration to use when running AOT compilation.
The type to perform the AOT compilation on.
The AOT class. Add this C# code to your project.
member.returns: True if AOT compilation was successful.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsAotCompilationManager.AddAotCompilation(System.Type,Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsMetaProperty[],System.Boolean)
member.summary: Adds a new AOT compilation unit.
member.param: The type of object we are AOT compiling.
The members on the object which will be serialized/deserialized.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsAotCompilationManager.GenerateDirectConverterForTypeInCSharp(System.Type,Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsMetaProperty[],System.Boolean)
member.summary: AOT compiles the object (in C#).

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsReflectionUtility.GetInterface(System.Type,System.Type)
member.summary: Searches for a particular implementation of the given interface type inside of the type. This is particularly useful if the interface type is an open type, ie, typeof(IFace{}), because this method will then return IFace{} but with appropriate type parameters inserted.
member.param: The base type to search for interface
The interface type to search for. Can be an open generic type.
member.returns: The actual interface type that the type contains, or null if there is no implementation of the given interfaceType on type.

member.name: T:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsWeakReferenceConverter
member.summary: Serializes and deserializes WeakReferences.

member.name: T:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsMetaType
member.summary: MetaType contains metadata about a type. This is used by the reflection serializer.

member.name: P:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsMetaType.HasDefaultConstructor
member.summary: Returns true if the type represented by this metadata contains a default constructor.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsMetaType.EmitAotData
member.summary: Attempt to emit an AOT compiled direct converter for this type.
member.returns: True if AOT data was emitted, false otherwise.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsMetaType.CreateInstance
member.summary: Creates a new instance of the type that this metadata points back to. If this type has a default constructor, then Activator.CreateInstance will be used to construct the type (or Array.CreateInstance if it an array). Otherwise, an uninitialized object created via FormatterServices.GetSafeUninitializedObject is used to construct the instance.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsMetaType.ClearCache
member.summary: Clears out the cached type results. Useful if some prior assumptions become invalid, ie, the default member serialization mode.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsMetaType.CanSerializeProperty(Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsConfig,System.Reflection.PropertyInfo,System.Reflection.MemberInfo[],System.Boolean)
member.summary: Returns if the given property should be serialized.
member.param: Should a property without any annotations be serialized?

member.name: T:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsResult
member.summary: The result of some sort of operation. A result is either successful or not, but if it is successful then there may be a set of warnings/messages associated with it. These warnings describe the performed error recovery operations.

member.name: F:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsResult._success
member.summary: Is this result successful?
member.remarks: This is intentionally a `success` state so that when the object is default constructed it defaults to a failure state.

member.name: F:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsResult._messages
member.summary: The warning or error messages associated with the result. This may be null if there are no messages.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsResult.AddMessage(System.String)
member.summary: Adds a new message to this result.
member.param: The new message to be added.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsResult.AddMessages(Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsResult)
member.summary: Adds only the messages from the other result into this result, ignoring the success/failure status of the other result.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsResult.Merge(Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsResult)
member.summary: Merges the other result into this one. If the other result failed, then this one too will have failed.
member.remarks: Note that you can use += instead of this method so that you don't bury the actual method call that is generating the other fsResult.

member.name: F:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsResult.Success
member.summary: A successful result.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsResult.Warn(System.String)
member.summary: Create a result that is successful but contains the given warning message.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsResult.Fail(System.String)
member.summary: Create a result that failed.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsResult.op_Addition(Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsResult,Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsResult)
member.summary: Only use this as +=!

member.name: P:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsResult.Failed
member.summary: Did this result fail? If so, you can see the reasons why in `RawMessages`.

member.name: P:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsResult.Succeeded
member.summary: Was the result a success? Note that even successful operations may have warning messages (`RawMessages`) associated with them.

member.name: P:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsResult.HasWarnings
member.summary: Does this result have any warnings? This says nothing about if it failed or succeeded, just if it has warning messages associated with it.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsResult.AssertSuccess
member.summary: A simply utility method that will assert that this result is successful. If it is not, then an exception is thrown.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsResult.AssertSuccessWithoutWarnings
member.summary: A simple utility method that will assert that this result is successful and that there are no warning messages. This throws an exception if either of those asserts are false.

member.name: P:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsResult.AsException
member.summary: Utility method to convert the result to an exception. This method is only defined is `Failed` returns true.

member.name: T:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsIEnumerableConverter
member.summary: Provides serialization support for anything which extends from `IEnumerable` and has an `Add` method.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsIEnumerableConverter.GetElementType(System.Type)
member.summary: Fetches the element type for objects inside of the collection.

member.name: T:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsNullableConverter
member.summary: The reflected converter will properly serialize nullable types. However, we do it here instead as we can emit less serialization data.

member.name: T:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsDateConverter
member.summary: Supports serialization for DateTime, DateTimeOffset, and TimeSpan.

member.name: T:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsMemberSerialization
member.summary: Controls how the reflected converter handles member serialization.

member.name: F:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsMemberSerialization.OptIn
member.summary: Only members with [SerializeField] or [fsProperty] attributes are serialized.

member.name: F:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsMemberSerialization.OptOut
member.summary: Only members with [NotSerialized] or [fsIgnore] will not be serialized.

member.name: F:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsMemberSerialization.Default
member.summary: The default member serialization behavior is applied.

member.name: F:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsGlobalConfig.IsCaseSensitive
member.summary: Should deserialization be case sensitive? If this is false and the JSON has multiple members with the same keys only separated by case, then this results in undefined behavior.

member.name: F:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsGlobalConfig.AllowInternalExceptions
member.summary: If exceptions are allowed internally, then additional date formats can be deserialized. Note that the Full Serializer public API will *not* throw exceptions with this enabled; errors will still be returned in a fsResult instance.

member.name: F:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsGlobalConfig.InternalFieldPrefix
member.summary: This string will be used to prefix fields used internally by FullSerializer.

member.name: T:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsConfig
member.summary: Enables some top-level customization of Full Serializer.

member.name: F:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsConfig.SerializeAttributes
member.summary: The attributes that will force a field or property to be serialized.

member.name: F:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsConfig.IgnoreSerializeAttributes
member.summary: The attributes that will force a field or property to *not* be serialized.

member.name: F:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsConfig.DefaultMemberSerialization
member.summary: The default member serialization.

member.name: F:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsConfig.GetJsonNameFromMemberName
member.summary: Convert a C# field/property name into the key used for the JSON object. For example, you could force all JSON names to lowercase with: fsConfig.GetJsonNameFromMemberName = (name, info) => name.ToLower(); This will only be used when the name is not explicitly specified with fsProperty.

member.name: F:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsConfig.EnablePropertySerialization
member.summary: If false, then *all* property serialization support will be disabled - even properties explicitly annotated with fsProperty or any other opt-in annotation. Setting this to false means that SerializeNonAutoProperties and SerializeNonPublicSetProperties will be completely ignored.

member.name: F:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsConfig.SerializeNonAutoProperties
member.summary: Should the default serialization behaviour include non-auto properties?

member.name: F:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsConfig.SerializeNonPublicSetProperties
member.summary: Should the default serialization behaviour include properties with non-public setters?

member.name: F:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsConfig.CustomDateTimeFormatString
member.summary: If not null, this string format will be used for DateTime instead of the default one.

member.name: F:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsConfig.Serialize64BitIntegerAsString
member.summary: Int64 and UInt64 will be serialized and deserialized as string for compatibility

member.name: F:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsConfig.SerializeEnumsAsInteger
member.summary: Enums are serialized using their names by default. Setting this to true will serialize them as integers instead.

member.name: T:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsContext
member.summary: fsContext stores global metadata that can be used to customize how fsConverters operate during serialization.

member.name: F:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsContext._contextObjects
member.summary: All of the context objects.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsContext.Reset
member.summary: Removes all context objects from the context.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsContext.Set``1(``0)
member.summary: Sets the context object for the given type with the given value.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsContext.Has``1
member.summary: Returns true if there is a context object for the given type.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsContext.Get``1
member.summary: Fetches the context object for the given type.

member.name: F:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsSerializer._availableConverters
member.summary: Converters that can be used for type registration.

member.name: F:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsSerializer._availableDirectConverters
member.summary: Direct converters (optimized _converters). We use these so we don't have to perform a scan through every item in _converters and can instead just do an O(1) lookup. This is potentially important to perf when there are a ton of direct converters.

member.name: F:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsSerializer._processors
member.summary: Processors that are available.

member.name: F:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsSerializer._references
member.summary: Reference manager for cycle detection.

member.name: F:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsSerializer._abstractTypeRemap
member.summary: Allow the user to provide default storage types for interfaces and abstract classes. For example, a model could have IList{int} as a parameter, but the serialization data does not specify a List{int} type. A IList{} -> List{} remapping will cause List{} to be used as the default storage type. see https://github.com/jacobdufault/fullserializer/issues/120 for additional context.

member.name: F:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsSerializer._cachedConverterTypeInstances
member.summary: Converter type to converter instance lookup table. This could likely be stored inside from serialized type to converter.

member.name: F:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsSerializer._cachedConverters
member.summary: A cache from type to it's converter.

member.name: F:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsSerializer._cachedProcessors
member.summary: A cache from type to the set of processors that are interested in it.

member.name: F:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsSerializer.Context
member.summary: A context object that fsConverters can use to customize how they operate.

member.name: F:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsSerializer.Config
member.summary: Configuration options. Also see fsGlobalConfig.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsSerializer.AddProcessor(Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsObjectProcessor)
member.summary: Add a new processor to the serializer. Multiple processors can run at the same time in the same order they were added in.
member.param: The processor to add.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsSerializer.RemoveProcessor``1
member.summary: Remove all processors which derive from TProcessor.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsSerializer.SetDefaultStorageType(System.Type,System.Type)
member.summary: Provide a default storage type for the given abstract or interface type. If a type is deserialized which contains an interface/abstract field type and a mapping is provided, the mapped type will be used by default. For example, IList{T} => List{T} or IDictionary{TKey, TValue} => Dictionary{TKey, TValue}.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsSerializer.GetProcessors(System.Type)
member.summary: Fetches all of the processors for the given type.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsSerializer.AddConverter(Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsBaseConverter)
member.summary: Adds a new converter that can be used to customize how an object is serialized and deserialized.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsSerializer.GetConverter(System.Type,System.Type)
member.summary: Fetches a converter that can serialize/deserialize the given type.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsSerializer.TrySerialize``1(``0,Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsData@)
member.summary: Helper method that simply forwards the call to TrySerialize(typeof(T), instance, out data);

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsSerializer.TryDeserialize``1(Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsData,``0@)
member.summary: Generic wrapper around TryDeserialize that simply forwards the call.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsSerializer.TrySerialize(System.Type,System.Object,Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsData@)
member.summary: Serialize the given value.
member.param: The type of field/property that stores the object instance. This is important particularly for inheritance, as a field storing an IInterface instance should have type information included.
The actual object instance to serialize.
The serialized state of the object.
member.returns: If serialization was successful.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsSerializer.TrySerialize(System.Type,System.Type,System.Object,Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsData@)
member.summary: Serialize the given value.
member.param: The type of field/property that stores the object instance. This is important particularly for inheritance, as a field storing an IInterface instance should have type information included.
An fsBaseConverter derived type that will be used to serialize the object instead of the converter found via the normal discovery mechanisms.
The actual object instance to serialize.
The serialized state of the object.
member.returns: If serialization was successful.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsSerializer.TryDeserialize(Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsData,System.Type,System.Object@)
member.summary: Attempts to deserialize a value from a serialized state.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsSerializer.TryDeserialize(Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsData,System.Type,System.Type,System.Object@)
member.summary: Attempts to deserialize a value from a serialized state.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsSerializer.EnsureDictionary(Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsData)
member.summary: Ensures that the data is a dictionary. If it is not, then it is wrapped inside of one.

member.name: T:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsSerializer.fsLazyCycleDefinitionWriter
member.summary: This manages instance writing so that we do not write unnecessary $id fields. We only need to write out an $id field when there is a corresponding $ref field. This is able to write $id references lazily because the fsData instance is not actually written out to text until we have entirely finished serializing it.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsSerializer.IsReservedKeyword(System.String)
member.summary: Returns true if the given key is a special keyword that full serializer uses to add additional metadata on top of the emitted JSON.

member.name: F:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsSerializer.Key_ObjectReference
member.summary: This is an object reference in part of a cyclic graph.

member.name: F:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsSerializer.Key_ObjectDefinition
member.summary: This is an object definition, as part of a cyclic graph.

member.name: F:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsSerializer.Key_InstanceType
member.summary: This specifies the actual type of an object (the instance type was different from the field type).

member.name: F:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsSerializer.Key_Version
member.summary: The version string for the serialized data.

member.name: F:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsSerializer.Key_Content
member.summary: If we have to add metadata but the original serialized state was not a dictionary, then this will contain the original data.

member.name: F:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsSerializer.Key_UnitDefault
member.summary: Dictionary for the default values of each of the unit's value port.

member.name: F:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsSerializer.Key_UnitPosition
member.summary: Specifies the position of a unit in its respective graph.

member.name: F:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsSerializer.Key_UnitGuid
member.summary: Unique guid of the unit instance.

member.name: F:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsSerializer.Key_UnitFormerType
member.summary: Meant for 'missing type' units to remember the type of node they are supposed to be. This will allow us to try and convert back if the correct type becomes available again.

member.name: F:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsSerializer.Key_UnitFormerValue
member.summary: Meant for 'missing type' units to remember the state of the node they are supposed to be. This will allow to preserve the serialized instance values of the node when its type was lost.

member.name: F:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsSerializer.TypeName_Unit
member.summary: Hard-coded fully qualified name of the 'unit' type.

member.name: F:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsSerializer.Type_Unit
member.summary: The 'Unit' type defined in VS. Made static as to only be deserialized once and potentially used multiple times.

member.name: F:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsSerializer.TypeName_MissingType
member.summary: Hard-coded fully qualified name of the 'missing type unit' type.

member.name: F:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsSerializer.Type_MissingType
member.summary: The 'MissingType' type defined in VS. Made static as to only be deserialized once and potentially used multiple times.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsSerializer.IsVisualScriptingUnit(Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsData)
member.summary:
Makes a guess as to if the data represents a VS unit.
If this turns out to be too general and objects that are not meant to be units get converted to MissingType units, consider adding a dedicated serialized field exclusive to units as to be able to check against it. (Something named like "isVisualScriptingUnit")

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsSerializer.StripDeserializationMetadata(Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsData@)
member.summary: Strips all deserialization metadata from the object, like $type and $content fields.
member.remarks: After making this call, you will *not* be able to deserialize the same object instance. The metadata is strictly necessary for deserialization!

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsSerializer.ConvertLegacyData(Unity.VisualScripting.FullSerializer.fsData@)
member.summary: This function converts legacy serialization data into the new format, so that the import process can be unified and ignore the old format.

member.name: T:Unity.VisualScripting.EventBus
member.summary: A global container that maps Event names and Component references to actions for registered listeners.

remarks.para: It is the EventMachine base class for ScriptMachine and StateMachine that triggers events. This overrides almost all Unity callbacks (such as Awake, OnEnable, Update, etc.) and triggers an event on the EventBus.

example.para:
The following example shows how to use the EventBus to send a custom event from a script to a node in a graph. It also shows how to use the EventBus as a global event manager by executing a callback in a script, not just a node.
For more information on how to create custom event nodes refer to the User Manual.
In this example we've added some code to a GameObject. This code checks for when the user presses a sequence of keys to enable a cheat code, then triggers the CheatCodeActivated event. We register the CheatCodeActivated event in the Start method. The Update method triggers the event twice with 2 different targets: one for the CheatCodeActivated callback and the other to trigger the CheatCodeEnabled Node.

member.name: T:Unity.VisualScripting.MemberInfoComparer
member.summary: A member info comparer that will ignore the ReflectedType property by relying on the metadata token for comparison.

member.name: T:Unity.VisualScripting.TypeFilter
member.summary: Filters the list of types displayed in the inspector drawer.

member.name: T:Unity.VisualScripting.ValueAttribute
member.summary: Allows the customisation of the SystemObjectInspector by displaying just the value

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.AttributeUtility.GetAttributeOfEnumMember``1(System.Enum)
member.summary: Gets attributes on an enum member, eg. enum E { [Attr] A }

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.MemberUtility.MakeGenericMethodVia(System.Reflection.MethodInfo,System.Type[])
member.remarks: This may return an open-constructed method as well.

member.name: T:Unity.VisualScripting.Singleton`1
member.remarks: Does not support objects hidden with hide flags.

member.name: T:Unity.VisualScripting.VariablesSaver
member.summary: Listens to the OnApplicationQuit on OnApplicationPause hooks to trigger the serialization of saved variables into PlayerPrefs.

member.name: T:Unity.VisualScripting.SceneSingleton`1
member.remarks: Does not support objects hidden with hide flags.

member.name: T:Unity.VisualScripting.ISet`1
member.summary: Generic collection that guarantees the uniqueness of its elements, as defined by a comparer. It also supports basic set operations such as Union, Intersection, Difference, and Symmetric Difference.
member.typeparam: The type of elements contained in the set.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.ISet`1.Add(`0)
member.summary: Adds the specified item to the set.
member.param: The item to add to the set.
member.returns: true if the item was added to the set (i.e., it was not already present); otherwise, false.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.ISet`1.UnionWith(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{`0})
member.summary: Modifies the current set to contain all elements that are present in either the current set or the specified collection.
member.param: The collection to compare to the current set.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.ISet`1.IntersectWith(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{`0})
member.summary: Modifies the current set to contain only elements that are present in both the current set and the specified collection.
member.param: The collection to compare to the current set.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.ISet`1.ExceptWith(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{`0})
member.summary: Modifies the current set to contain only elements that are not present in the specified collection.
member.param: The collection to compare to the current set.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.ISet`1.SymmetricExceptWith(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{`0})
member.summary: Modifies the current set to contain only elements that are present either in the current set or in the specified collection, but not both.
member.param: The collection to compare to the current set.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.ISet`1.IsSubsetOf(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{`0})
member.summary: Determines whether the current set is a subset of the specified collection.
member.param: The collection to compare to the current set.
member.returns: true if the current set is a subset of the specified collection; otherwise, false.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.ISet`1.IsSupersetOf(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{`0})
member.summary: Determines whether the current set is a superset of the specified collection.
member.param: The collection to compare to the current set.
member.returns: true if the current set is a superset of the specified collection; otherwise, false.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.ISet`1.IsProperSubsetOf(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{`0})
member.summary: Determines whether the current set is a proper subset of the specified collection. A proper subset is a subset that is not equal to the other set.
member.param: The collection to compare to the current set.
member.returns: true if the current set is a proper subset of the specified collection; otherwise, false.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.ISet`1.IsProperSupersetOf(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{`0})
member.summary: Determines whether the current set is a proper superset of the specified collection. A proper superset is a superset that is not equal to the other set.
member.param: The collection to compare to the current set.
member.returns: true if the current set is a proper superset of the specified collection; otherwise, false.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.ISet`1.Overlaps(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{`0})
member.summary: Determines whether the current set has any elements in common with the specified collection.
member.param: The collection to compare to the current set.
member.returns: true if the current set and the specified collection share at least one common element; otherwise, false.

member.name: M:Unity.VisualScripting.ISet`1.SetEquals(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{`0})
member.summary: Determines whether the current set and the specified collection contain the same elements.
member.param: The collection to compare to the current set.
member.returns: true if the current set and the specified collection contain the same elements; otherwise, false.

member.name: F:Unity.VisualScripting.VariableKind.Flow
member.summary: Temporary variables local to the execution flow.

member.name: F:Unity.VisualScripting.VariableKind.Graph
member.summary: Variables local to the current graph.

member.name: F:Unity.VisualScripting.VariableKind.Object
member.summary: Variables shared across the current game object.

member.name: F:Unity.VisualScripting.VariableKind.Scene
member.summary: Variables shared across the current scene.

member.name: F:Unity.VisualScripting.VariableKind.Application
member.summary: Variables shared across scenes. These will be reset when the application quits.

member.name: F:Unity.VisualScripting.VariableKind.Saved
member.summary: Variables that persist even after the application quits. Unity object references are not supported.
